name: Curried Chicken Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. To prepare the onion, peel the skin from one half of the onion and set it aside for another use. Cut the remaining half in half through the root. Place the cut side down on the cutting board. Hold the knife horizontally and make cuts parallel to the board, almost to the root end. Next, make vertical cuts lengthwise, then slice across the cuts to the root end (The closer the cuts are spaced, the finer the onion will be chopped).
2. Melt margarine in a large skillet over medium heat until foamy. Add the cut onions and cook, stirring occasionally, for about 3 minutes or until they are soft. Remove from heat.
3. Stir together rice, raisins, parsley, curry powder, brown sugar, poultry seasoning, and garlic powder with the onion mixture; mix well and set aside.
4. Flatten each chicken breast to a thickness of 3/8 inch. Season both sides with salt and pepper.
5. Divide the rice mixture evenly among the chicken breasts, spreading it to within 1 inch of the edges.
6. Roll up each chicken breast from the short end in a jelly-roll style, ensuring that the filling is completely enclosed. Secure each roll with wooden toothpicks.
7. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the rolled chicken pieces in a single layer and cook for 15 minutes or until browned on all sides. Pour in white wine and chicken base; stir carefully until dissolved. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
